_comment: Read-only levers/thresholds for the /doctor Step 4 optimization-eval arm (W6, ADR-110/111). The eval-arm READS this file and NEVER writes it — it detects threshold crossings over the existing telemetry ledgers and auto-logs ONE inbox ticket per finding into docs/step-1-ideas/needs-shaping/ (DEFER- prefix for deferral-class). It NEVER mutates the levers/thresholds here; tuning is hand-managed by the infra author. Keys are grouped by the roll-up seam the eval-arm reads (no second parser is authored — it reuses infra-doctor.sh --tokens over _fable-spend.jsonl and metrics-summary.sh --json over _metrics.jsonl).

fable_spend._notes: per_dispatch_in/out match the ADR-088 D4 over_envelope envelope (in>90k OR out>4k). over_envelope_count_ceiling 0 means: any flagged over_envelope dispatch is a finding.

run_metrics._notes: duration_seconds is the field T11 added to measure-run.sh (null-skip preserved by the aggregator). A median over the ceiling is a mis-calibrated-threshold / regression finding.

measurement_gaps._source: metrics-summary.sh --json — the per-metric n vs runs
measurement_gaps._notes: An aggregator metric whose n is below min_runs_for_signal, or whose null fraction exceeds max_null_fraction, is a measurement-gap finding (the lever is not actually being measured).

unfired_levers._notes: A ledger with no new entries within max_idle_days is an unfired-lever finding (the optimization seam exists but nothing is flowing through it).
